title: Patriots, Kraft Group sue Foxborough over annual stadium licensing fees
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: New England Patriots Plaintiffs allege town officials imposed nearly $1 million in new charges tied to the Gillette Stadium events.
Robert Kraft, owner of the Patriots, at the NFL Super Bowl in February.

AP Photo/Frank Franklin II Kraft Sports & Entertainment and the New England Patriots have filed a lawsuit against the Town of Foxborough over new financial obligations tied to Gillette Stadium’s entertainment license.

title: Celtics columnist: Miami has the edge in Giannis Antetokounmpo chase

Bucks' Giannis Antetokounmpo warming up before a game in March, 2026.

title: 3 New England winners take home top honors at the 2026 James Beard Awards
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Food News Providence's Loma, Portland restaurateur Dana Street, and Dover chef Evan Hennessey earned top honors at the restaurant industry's biggest awards night.
Providence, RI.

07/09/24 - Champagne-style cocktail with red beet sugar cube, amaro sweet vermouth, and sparkling wine at LOMA, a Latin-American influenced bar and lounge close to Depasquale Plaza off Atwells Avenue in Providence.

title: Celebrations spill onto streets of Brockton after Cape Verde’s surprise World Cup draw
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News Rowdy crowds led to at least nine arrests following Monday’s match, according to Brockton police.
Cape Verde fans celebrate their country's tie with Spain on Monday in Brockton.

Suzanne Kreiter/Boston Globe Staff By Abby Patkin June 16, 2026 | 3:11 PM 1 minute to read As excited Cape Verde fans spilled out onto Brockton’s streets Monday to celebrate their soccer team’s stunning World Cup draw against Spain, local police say some revelers turned a little too rowdy.

Estella Restaurant in downtown Boston.

title: Scratch ticket yields $15 million win in Millis

A trust in Quincy claimed a $15 million grand prize in the “Diamond Deluxe” scratch-off ticket game last week.

title: Could Cambridge require a 30-minute wait between drinks?
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Breweries & Bars An early draft regulation raised concerns among business owners and patrons, but city officials said no changes have been adopted.
A Milk Stout from the Left Hand Brewing Company pours from one of the 40 taps on the bar at Lord Hobo.

JOSH REYNOLDS FOR THE BOSTON GLOBE A draft overhaul of Cambridge’s alcohol licensing regulations is drawing pushback from mostly patrons and some local bar and restaurant owners, over a proposal requiring patrons to wait 30 minutes between alcoholic drinks.
